message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Nobu Restaurant (Indian Wells) (Indian Wells)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 148 reviews; Restaurant Guru aggregates ~974; Google rating reported ~4.2 [UNVERIFIED exact count]
- Sentiment: mostly positive with mixed notes on price/noise/service consistency
- Longevity: Originated as a 2014 BNP Paribas Open pop-up; permanent fixture since February 2023 (~3 years as permanent)
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life editorial feature ('feels like a secret sushi club')","Featured on OpenTable Best Fine Dining Palm Springs/Palm Desert Cities list","Part of Chef Nobu Matsuhisa global brand (Matsuhisa is a James Beard Award winner, Best California Chef 2002 — credential belongs to chef/brand, not specifically this location)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best fine dining Indian Wells', 'Japanese restaurant Palm Desert/Indian Wells', 'date night Indian Wells', 'sushi Coachella Valley'. Dominates Indian Wells fine-dining query results alongside The Nest and Vue Grille & Bar.
- Site structured data: Official site noburestaurants.com/indian-wells presents menu structure and address but no visible schema.org markup detected in fetched content. Hours buried behind navigation rather than surfaced directly. Social handles (Facebook/Instagram @nobuindianwells) present. Brand-level site architecture rather than location-optimized for agent parsing.
- Visibility gap score: 2
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Indian Wells, California as of 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations:

**Nobu Indian Wells**
Highly anticipated by both residents and food enthusiasts, Nobu Indian Wells brought a new level of fine dining to the area with its global reputation for innovative Japanese-Peruvian fusion cuisine. The restaurant is known for dishes like black cod miso, yellowtail sashimi with jalapeño, and rock shrimp tempura.

**Kestrel A Richard Blais Kitchen + Lounge**
Located on the top floor of a striking and luxurious multipurpose clubhouse at the Indian Wells Golf Resort, the Kestrel A Richard Blais Kitchen + Lounge features celebrated chef and TV personality Richard Blais imparts his Left Coast take on American classics, signature cuts and coastal catches in a fresh, California-modern space.

**The Pink Cabana**
The Pink Cabana is an ingredient-driven restaurant featuring Mediterranean cuisine with a Moroccan influence, designed by Martyn Lawrence Bullard as a fresh modern take on the great tennis and racquet clubs of the 50's and 60's in Palm Springs.

**Frank's Place Restaurant & Lounge**
Frank's Place specializes in fine American-Continental cuisine prepared with the freshest ingredients and features an exceptional wine cellar.

**The Nest Restaurant & Nightclub**
The Nest is Indian Wells' most famous restaurant and lounge, serving fine guests, celebrities, golf and tennis legends since 1965.

These establishments offer upscale dining experiences ranging from Japanese-Peruvian fusion to contemporary American and Mediterranean cuisine.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Nobu Indian Wells and the BNP Paribas Open 2014:

The award-winning restaurant debuted at the 2014 BNP Paribas Open as part of the expansive venue construction project that resulted in a permanent Stadium 2. More specifically, it was constructed in tandem with new Stadium 2 at Indian Wells Tennis Garden and completed just weeks before the 2014 BNP Paribas Open.

Nobu makes it first foray into the desert, joining Chop House and Piero's Pizza Vino at the BNP Paribas Open's new Stadium 2 at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden. Nobu has 140 seats in a space designed by Severine Tatangelo, one of the founders of Studio PCH, a Malibu-based architectural design and development firm.

Additionally, Nobu Indian Wells permanently opened on a year-round basis to the general public beginning on Monday February 13, 2023, with the world-renowned restaurant opening its doors for Coachella Valley visitors and residents to enjoy beyond the two weeks of the BNP Paribas Open.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Nobu Indian Wells restaurant reviews and ratings:

## Rating Summary

**Google Rating:** Google users rate this restaurant 4.2 stars.

**OpenTable Rating:** Nobu Indian Wells is rated 4.7 stars by 771 OpenTable diners.

**Other Platform Ratings:**
- Restaurant Guru rates it 4.5 out of 5 based on 974 reviews
- One source shows a 4.1-star rating from 114 customer reviews

## Review Highlights

The reviews are mixed. Positive reviews praise the food and ambiance:
- Nobu offers a "spectacular" dining experience with "amazing food," "fantastic service," and "beautiful presentation" of dishes
- Dishes such as black cod miso and crispy shiitake salad are highly recommended, and Nobu Indian Wells continues to be a top choice for great Japanese food and a special dining experience

However, some negative reviews mention service inconsistencies:
- Some guests reported poor and slow service, with drinks and food forgotten, though food quality was good
- Some diners noted that the arrival experience has changed for the worse, with no valet acknowledgment or warm welcomes despite being regular guests

Based on the search results, I found the Palm Springs Life article about Nobu Indian Wells. Here's what I discovered:

Nobu opened as a pop-up during the 2014 BNP Paribas Open and became a permanent fixture in February 2023. This high-end sushi haven is located atop Stadium 2 at the Indian Wells Tennis Garden.

The article's description captures the "secret sushi club" atmosphere quite well. Upon entering, "light and warmth and laughter spilled out — like we had discovered a secret club hidden away in the darkness."

Regarding the cuisine, Nobu — a high-end chain that began in New York as a collaboration between chef Nobu Matsuhisa, Robert De Niro, and Meir Teper — serves a menu that fuses traditional Japanese fare with South American flair. For the true Nobu experience, servers advise diners to order a mix of cold plates, hot dishes, sushi, and dessert (in that order).

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Japanese and sushi restaurants in the Coachella Valley:

## Top Recommendations

**Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar** (La Quinta)
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar in La Quinta offers a truly exceptional Japanese Speciality experience, blending authentic Japanese flavors with a contemporary California twist. Okura is the only sushi restaurant in the region to receive daily deliveries of Grade A fish and seafood coming in from Japan. Guests rave about the "fresh and delicious" sushi, with standout dishes like the Hamachi Jalapeno rolls and sashimi platters praised for their "high quality" and "stunning presentation."

**Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey** (Palm Springs)
If you're searching for refined, chef-driven sushi in Palm Springs, Sandfish Sushi sets the standard. Helmed by Chef Engin Onural, this sleek, modern restaurant blends traditional Japanese technique with subtle global influence. The menu features inventive sushi rolls, perfectly seared fish, and unique flavor combinations that set Sandfish apart as a top-tier dining destination.

**Ponzu Sushi** (Palm Springs)
Ponzu Sushi offers a welcoming setting and a broad menu that balances classic sushi staples with creative house rolls. It's especially popular for takeout and relaxed dine-in experiences.

**Otori Sushi** (Cathedral City)
With its fresh fish and relaxed vibe, Otori Sushi is a go-to destination for sushi enthusiasts in the valley.

**Misaki Sushi & Grill**
Misaki Sushi & Grill is a hidden gem nestled in Coachella Valley, offering a delightful fusion of traditional Japanese flavors and contemporary culinary creativity. As one of the top sushi spots in the region, Misaki is known for its fresh and expertly crafted sushi offerings.

## Recommendation
The restaurant would benefit from implementing Restaurant schema (schema.org) to improve local SEO visibility and provide search engines with standardized business information including hours, address, and contact details.

content.text: I have enough verification data. Let me synthesize findings.

confirms: Yelp 148 reviews confirmed (Yelp listing title: '394 Photos & 148 Reviews')
Restaurant Guru ~974 reviews confirmed (4.5/5 across 974 reviews)
Originated as 2014 BNP Paribas Open pop-up — confirmed (debuted at 2014 BNP Paribas Open with new Stadium 2)
Permanent year-round opening February 13, 2023 — confirmed exactly
Palm Springs Life feature 'feels like a secret sushi club' confirmed (article title: 'Nobu at Indian Wells Tennis Garden Feels Like a Secret Sushi Club')
Featured on OpenTable Best Fine Dining Palm Springs/Palm Desert Cities Indian Wells list — confirmed
Sentiment characterization (mostly positive with mixed notes on service consistency) — supported by review excerpts noting slow service / forgotten orders alongside praise
Surfaces strongly for 'best fine dining Indian Wells' query — confirmed (appears prominently in TripAdvisor, OpenTable, City of Indian Wells listings)

contradicts: Claim that Nobu 'dominates Indian Wells fine-dining query results alongside The Nest and Vue Grille & Bar' — partially contradicted. Independent fine-dining lists for Indian Wells pair Nobu with The Nest, Kestrel (Richard Blais), The Pink Cabana, and Frank's Place — Vue Grille & Bar does not consistently appear as the primary co-anchor in independent search results.

Claim that Nobu 'dominates' the 'sushi Coachella Valley' query is overstated — independent sushi/Japanese queries for the Coachella Valley surface Okura Robata (La Quinta), Sandfish (Palm Springs), Ponzu, Otori, and Misaki as top picks; Nobu does not appear in the top recommendations on TripAdvisor/OpenTable sushi-specific lists for the valley. Nobu surfaces in Japanese fine-dining contexts, not generic 'best sushi' lists.
flagged_as_unverified: ~3 years as permanent fixture — arithmetic-correct (Feb 2023 to June 2026 ≈ 3.3 years) but framing 'as permanent' since the brand had pop-up presence since 2014 is accurate
Visibility gap score of 2 — this is a proprietary scoring rubric and cannot be independently verified
Convertibility tier 'anchor-reference' — proprietary classification, cannot externally verify

Matsuhisa James Beard 'Best California Chef 2002' specific credential — not directly verified in this search (the credential is attributed to chef/brand, not location, which the assessment already notes)
input.notes: Core factual claims (review counts, longevity timeline, editorial credentials, structured-data absence) check out cleanly against independent sources. The main weakness is the claim that Nobu 'dominates' sushi/Coachella Valley queries — Nobu surfaces strongly in Indian Wells fine-dining and Japanese fine-dining contexts but is overshadowed in generic 'best sushi Coachella Valley' results by Okura, Sandfish, and Ponzu. Pairing with 'Vue Grille & Bar' as a co-anchor is also weakly supported; Kestrel and The Pink Cabana are more consistent co-occurrences. The structured-data assessment is accurate — noburestaurants.com/indian-wells has no JSON-LD or microdata, and hours sit behind a separate navigation link, confirming the brand-level (not location-optimized) architecture critique.
